review_comment: ideally we would have used the apiset 😉

(Going back and forth between these two threads -- SystemFunction036 is hosted in `api-ms-win-security-systemfunctions-l1-1-0`... but that apiset is only documented as containing SystemFunction036 after contract version `win8`. I bet they could back-version that and be fine, but that's not likely to happen.)

Since the apiset doesn't exist on win8, why shouldn't we have used `advapi32.dll` itself? The loader will take care of finding the secret place where it actually lives.

Also, why did we have to delay load this? I have so many questions.
I'm going to open a PR documenting the reasons behind this better.
What on earth? RtlGenRandom is fully documented, but we're.. looking it up with a weird name in a private DLL? _Why_?

**Interesting**. It's a macro.

It _does_ have a prototype in NTSecAPI.h, under this name. Could we really not have used it with the wil helper? It would resolve to `SystemFunction036`, sure, but at least we wouldn't leak that name into our own code.

If not, could we not have stringified the content of the RtlGenRandom macro instead?
